Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Let me start by understanding the standard Hire to Retire process and then analyze each model

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Standard Hire to Retire Process 1 Post Job Ad 2 Screen Candidates 3 Conduct Interviews 4 Make Hiring Decision 5 Onboard Employee 6 Add to Payroll 7 Close Case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The logical flow should be You need to screen candidates after posting a job You conduct interviews after screening You make a hiring decision after interviews You onboard the employee after hiring decision You add to payroll after onboarding You close the case at the end

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 1 Analysis Let me trace through the model Nodes Post Screen Decide Interview Onboard Payroll Close Edges Post Screen Screen Decide Screen Interview Decide Onboard Onboard Payroll Payroll Close

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the partial order is Post must come before Screen Screen must come before Decide Screen must come before Interview Decide must come before Onboard Onboard must come before Payroll Payroll must come before Close

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: This creates a problem Screen Decide AND Screen Interview but theres no constraint between Decide and Interview

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This means Interview could happen before Decide which is illogical

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Actually I need to think about this more carefully In a partial order if theres no constraint between two activities they could happen in any order or even in parallel

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So in Model 1 Screen must happen After Screen both Decide and Interview can happen no constraint between them After Decide Onboard must happen After Onboard Payroll must happen After Payroll Close must happen

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The anomalies in Model 1 Interview can happen before Make Hiring Decision This is illogical you should conduct interviews before making a hiring decision Interview has no successors The Interview activity doesnt lead to anything which is odd typically after interviews youd have a decision point

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 2 Analysis Nodes Post Screen Interview Decide loop onboarding xor payroll Close Edges Post Screen Post Interview Interview Decide Decide loop onboarding loop onboarding xor payroll xor payroll Close

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: After Post both Screen and Interview can happen no constraint between them Interview must happen before Decide Decide must happen before loop onboarding loop onboarding must happen before xor payroll xor payroll must happen before Close

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Anomalies in Model 2 Interview can happen before Screen This is illogical you should screen candidates before conducting interviews Screen has no successors Screen doesnt lead to anything meaningful loop onboarding The loop allows onboarding to repeat which might not be normative xor payroll with skip The XOR allows skipping payroll entirely which defeats the purpose of onboarding

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: I would say Model 1 is closer to normative because The core sequence Post Screen Decide Onboard Payroll Close is preserved The main issue is that Interview timing is flexible but the decision making still happens before onboarding Model 2 has the more fundamental problem of potentially conducting interviews before screening and allowing payroll to be skipped entirely
